name: Elke Sommer's Christmas Stollen
recipeIngredient: 1 package Active, dry, or cake yeast
recipeInstructions:
1. Make two or three days before serving.
2. In a medium bowl, dissolve yeast in water.
3. Stir in lukewarm milk, granulated sugar, salt, egg, soft butter or margarine.
4. Next, stir in slivered almonds, chopped walnuts, lemon peel, orange peel, raisins, 1 and 1/2 cups flour.
5. Mix with a spoon until smooth. Mix in enough of the remaining flour to handle easily.
6. Turn onto lightly floured board. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes.
7. Put into a greased bowl; brush top with melted butter and cover with towel.
8. Let rise in warm place (80-85°F) until double, about 1 and 1/2 hours.
9. Roll or pat dough into 12-inch by 8-inch oval.
10. Spread with 1 tbsp melted butter. Fold in two lengthwise into crescent.
11. Press edges firm enough to hold together; place on a greased sheet.
12. Brush top with remaining melted butter. Let rise until double, 35-45 minutes.
13. Heat oven to 375°F.
14. When loaf has doubled, bake 30-35 minutes or until golden brown and done.
15. Frost, while still warm, with confectioner's sugar and 1 tbsp milk, beaten together until creamy.
16. Let frosting drip down sides of loaf.
17. Decorate with glaced cherries and sliced almonds, if desired.
18. Store in tightly covered container.
